state.next-action:
Phase 42.1 is now shipped.
Completed in this session: 1. 42.1-01 - canonical config consolidation around `gad-config.toml`, with `.planning/config.json` regenerated as compatibility output 2. 42.1-02 - clarified `workspace` vs `worktree` vs `workstream` in CLI/docs and added first-class `gad worktree new` 3. 42.1-03 - reviewed live upstream GSD parity and ported stale/orphan worktree detection that fits GAD 4. 42.1-04 - retired `loop_compliance` in current self-eval output and replaced it with `framework_compliance` + `hydration` 5. 42.1-05 - locked the official framework comparison matrix to bare / GSD / GAD / emergent / custom with Escape the Dungeon as the canonical target 6. 42.1-06 - captured the brood-editor / local-first project-market follow-up and fixed it to local-dev-only in its first cut
Current measured reality after the metric shift: - framework compliance: 4 / 179 completed tasks fully attributed = 2.2% - hydration overhead: 1,521,173 estimated snapshot tokens / 2,471,819 total project tokens = 61.5% - interpretation: the old loop-compliance story was flattering the framework; the new metrics show that attribution discipline is still weak and hydration cost is very high
Immediate next queue: 1. 42.2-03 - rename `sdk/skills/gad-quick-skill/` -> `sdk/skills/create-proto-skill/` 2. 42.2-04 - validator v2 file-ref dedup fix (overlapping long/short path refs) 3. 42.2-05 - whitepaper-framed /findings index copy 4. 42.2-06 - implement create-proto-skill using bulk+checkpoint pattern (decision gad-171 — LOCKED, not "design" anymore) 5. phase 42.3 (NEW) - workflows as a first-class concept: hand-authored mermaid graphs + computed actual graphs + diff on /planning (decision gad-172) 6. phase 43 / 44 / 44.5 / 45 - species rename, marketplace, brood editor, and rebrand work
Cross-cutting follow-up logged from this closeout: - 35-10 - review SDK context-engine hydration policy now that canonical config and hydration metrics are both live; decide whether fixed file manifests should give way to scoped/query-driven context plus relevant decisions
Decisions locked this turn: - gad-171: create-proto-skill uses bulk batching with per-candidate checkpoints - gad-172: Workflows are a first-class GAD concept (hand-authored + computed + diff on /planning) - gad-173: Workflow validator = expected/actual graph diff = next framework-discipline metric. Answers all three 42.3 open questions: (a) workflows nest via `parent-workflow` frontmatter, rendered as mermaid subgraphs; (b) validator IS the diff, emits `workflow_conformance = (matching − extra − out_of_order) / expected` which UPGRADES the flat presence-based `framework_compliance` (2.2% baseline is on a metric we are replacing); (c) agents emit explicit `workflow_enter`/`workflow_exit` trace events as authoritative hints, sequence inference is the fallback.
Shipped this turn (phase 42.3 opening): - 42.3-01 done: `.planning/workflows/README.md` — schema, authoring rules, nesting, validator formula, trace event contract - 42.3-02 done: all six canonical workflows authored under `.planning/workflows/` — gad-loop, gad-discuss-plan-execute, gad-decide, gad-debug, gad-evolution, gad-findings. Nesting tree: gad-loop → {gad-discuss-plan-execute → {gad-decide, gad-debug}, gad-evolution, gad-findings}. - 42.3-06 done: three open design questions resolved in decision gad-173 - 42.3-07 done: remaining four workflows shipped alongside 42.3-02 - 42.3-08 new: rename `framework_compliance` → `workflow_conformance` in self-eval once synthesis + Workflows tab land
Phase 42.3 remaining: - 42.3-03 — build-site-data workflow extractor + WORKFLOWS in catalog - 42.3-04 — trace synthesis (actual graph from .gad-log events) - 42.3-05 — /planning Workflows tab with expected/actual/diff rendering - 42.3-08 — metric rename
Also tightened: - 44.5-02 dev-server bridge is now "accept any gad/npx in NODE_ENV=development" for the first cut. New task 44.5-02b captures the hardening pass (allow-list, arg sanitization) as a deferred follow-up.
